title: US-Israel Strikes on Iran Trigger Fears of Global Economic Crisis
content:
Before the US and Israel launched their recent attacks, experts had assessed that the Iranian regime was stagnating in the face of mass protests. At the heart of the current crisis is the fundamental structure and resilience of Iran’s deeply entrenched political system. In response to targeted decapitation strikes, the regime is expected to simply promote untested individuals from within its vast institutional layers. In the days immediately following the bombings, financial markets confidently bet that the economic fallout from this Middle Eastern excursion would be short-lived. Historically, such geopolitical flare-ups have tended to be brief, leading many investors to assume this conflict would be no exception.

Weeks later, however, the terrifying prospect of a drawn-out regional war is causing rapidly mounting economic problems across the globe. Oil prices have soared above $100 a barrel, European gas prices have doubled, and severe volatility stalks major financial markets. Consequently, consumers worldwide are bracing for a massive surge in living costs as inflation threatens to derail economic recovery. While some analysts maintain that the conflict will end quickly, others warn that stagflation could soon burst the current complacency bubble. With each passing day, new problems emerge, ranging from soaring petrol prices to the worst global travel disruption since the pandemic.

European heavy industry, still reeling from the 2022 energy price shock following Russia's invasion of Ukraine, is feeling the pinch acutely. Major chemical plants are actively raising prices or facing the very real threat of imminent closure. Furthermore, the cost of fertilizer—an important byproduct of the petroleum industry—is rising sharply and hurting farmers worldwide. Iran has aggressively threatened to send oil to $200 a barrel by targeting vital shipping lanes and regional pipelines. With Iranian missiles already hitting significant natural gas processing facilities, energy markets are seemingly on the road to a doomsday scenario.
